summary: Converted another block of Mario fighter code from assembly into C, moving several ftmario routines out of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s and into src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c, while updating ftmario.h prototypes. The PR also renamed the C-side wrapper at 0x800E0A00 from label-style lbl_800E0A00 to function-style func_800E0A00 and updated remaining assembly references to use the function symbol.
what_changed: Removed assembly implementations for func_800E0B00, func_800E0B48, func_800E0B90, func_800E0BE4, func_800E0C24, func_800E0C68, func_800E0CAC, and func_800E0CE0 from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s.
Added C implementations for the removed Mario routines in src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c.
Replaced an inline asm implementation named lbl_800E0A00 with a normal C function named func_800E0A00 that calls func_800E1368.
Updated remaining assembly references that stored the 0x800E0A00 callback address so they refer to func_800E0A00 instead of lbl_800E0A00.
Added extern declarations in ftmario.c for lbl_804D9190, lbl_804D9194, and lbl_803C5A20, used by newly decompiled routines.
Added ftmario.h prototypes for the newly decompiled functions.
smart_moves: The PR kept the symbol address stable while renaming lbl_800E0A00 to func_800E0A00, and patched the remaining assembly references to use the new function symbol. This avoids dangling label-style references while preserving callback behavior.
func_800E0B00 and func_800E0B48 were expressed directly as held-item checks through Fighter.x1974_heldItem followed by calls to func_80070CC4 or func_80070C48 only when func_8026B2B4 returns zero.

func_800E0BE4 was simplified from a manual word-copy loop into a struct assignment between ftMarioAttributes pointers, showing that the copied special-attribute block matches the known struct layout closely enough for C assignment.

The constants used by func_800E0C24 and func_800E0C68 were preserved as extern f32 labels, keeping the generated code tied to existing rodata/sdata labels rather than inventing semantic constant names without evidence.
The table lookup in func_800E0CE0 was expressed in terms of lbl_803C5A20[offset - 0xe], matching the assembly's load from a table base adjusted by the 0xe/0xf cases.
decomp_lessons: When assembly stores a callback address, converting the target to C may require updating still-assembled references from lbl_* to func_* so the symbol name matches the new C definition.
Short assembly wrappers that only call another function can often become ordinary C wrappers if the compiler emits the expected prologue/call/epilogue.
Manual paired word-copy loops over fighter attributes can sometimes be recovered as whole-struct assignment once the source and destination are identified as the same attributes type.

For fighter code, gobj->user_data commonly yields a Fighter*, and offsets such as x1974_heldItem, x10C_ftData, and x2D4_specialAttributes can anchor decompilation of otherwise generic pointer arithmetic.
Small action-state mapping helpers can be written with explicit conditionals or switch statements, but care is needed when default paths in assembly leave locals or outputs unchanged.
naming_conventions: Use function-style names like func_800E0A00 for callable code symbols instead of label-style names like lbl_800E0A00.
Mario fighter source and header symbols are kept under the ftMario/ftmario naming area, with public prototypes in src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.h.
Unknown data labels remain lbl_* when no semantic name is established, as with lbl_804D9190, lbl_804D9194, and lbl_803C5A20.
Existing fighter struct field names such as x1974_heldItem, x10C_ftData, x2D4_specialAttributes, and ext_attr were reused rather than replaced with speculative names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the 0x800E0A00 inline asm wrapper into a simple C call to func_800E1368.
Removed now-matched assembly blocks while leaving later unmatched ftmario assembly in place.
Preserved external float/data labels to control data references in functions calling func_800704F0 and indexing lbl_803C5A20.
Represented the assembly's copied 0x84-byte-ish Mario attributes block as `*sA = *ext_attr`, relying on the ftMarioAttributes struct layout to produce the same effect.
Used direct comparisons for the action values 9 and 10 in func_800E0CAC and a switch in func_800E0CE0 to reproduce action-to-result mappings 0xe and 0xf.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename func_800E0B00 through func_800E0CE0 to semantic Mario special-move names.
Search for uses of func_800E0A00 as a callback stored in Fighter fields x21DC or x21E4.
Compare other fighter character files for the same special-attributes copy pattern using ftData.ext_attr and x2D4_specialAttributes.
Investigate lbl_803C5A20 table semantics and whether the 0xe/0xf entries correspond to Mario action-state or item data.
